locationRefs: single door on the western promontory
sourceShortSummaries: The session recaps the party's arrival at Lord Endless Ending's manor and reestablishes everyone inside the ending party after a human guest is sent away.
The party compares what they know about the clock, the bargain, the road bodies, and the neighbor's house before deciding to approach Lord Endless Ending.
The party is formally presented to Lord Endless Ending and wins his welcome with carefully improvised mortal gifts.
Lord Endless Ending explains the neighbor's door, the zero-chiming clock, the 27th room, and the glass key that could undo his bargain.
The party refuses to bind itself to a fey bargain while still choosing to leave for the neighbor's house.
Before leaving, the party learns that the gate depends on their future choice and investigates the sleeping scholar's number-dreams and books.
Txarro tests the manor's river loop, and the party meets the awakened scholar Monetheus as he is sent toward the 27th house.
Monetheus realizes he is centuries out of time, explains Telestria's boundary work and brilliant-number maps, and leads the party toward the lone door.
sourceLongSummaries: The session opens by recapping the root tunnel from Dandelion House, the road bodies, the road guards, the ending party, the old halflings, the Rakshasa's story, and the clock striking zero as Lord Endless Ending sends a human guest toward the neighbor's house. The scene then pauses to place each character inside the manor: Edric is near the ballroom and protected against fey, Tarek has rejoined after a sideways architectural mishap and some theft, Txarro is poised between porch and ballroom with his owl following the departing woman, Ayveen stays close to the group, and Kaito returns from trying to find a better party vibe. Lord Endless Ending's beckoning of Edric is softened into an invitation to come over shortly, giving the party time to regroup.

Kaito asks what everyone learned while he was away from the Rakshasa conversation, and the party pieces together the working theory: when the clock chimes zero, Lord Endless Ending must send a mortal guest toward his neighbor's house, which may explain the headless bodies and the bad smell on the road. Txarro watches through his owl as the newly sent human woman heads north toward a western promontory rather than back toward the root house. The group reviews the naming contest that turned Lord Revel into Lord Endless Ending, the wizard neighbor's role, Holda's suspicious deed and elf contact, and the danger of the road guards, then decides to speak with Lord Endless Ending and improvise suitable gifts.

The party crosses the ballroom to Lord Endless Ending, an elegant, masked-seeming lord attended by an anxious badger butler. Kaito reads the butler's surface thoughts and finds a household servant desperate to clean, manage the house, and be released from waiting at his lord's side. The badger presents the group and asks what gifts they have brought; Txarro offers an eerie loon call from the mortal wilds, Edric awkwardly but sincerely gives holy water blessed by the Night Queen, Kaito turns a musical flourish into a showy song, Tarek performs a sleight-of-hand trick with a mouse skull, and Ayveen offers decorated sheet music for a moonrise composition. Lord Endless Ending answers each gift with poetry or judgment and finally welcomes them into his home, noting that he has rarely had the chance to greet mortals.

Txarro admits that the party is trapped in the realm, and Lord Endless Ending says there is a way home through his nearest neighbors' house to the north, the same direction the woman was sent. When the party connects her departure to the clock, the Lord confirms that when the grandfather clock strikes zero, it is time to show a guest home through the neighbor's house. He says he was tricked by a mortal wizard into an agreement he did not intend: to send mortal guests through that house whenever the clock strikes zero. Through portents he has learned of a cold-iron 27th room locked with a glass key holding the last light of twilight; if the key is returned to him and broken, the bargain will be undone. The party raises the road bodies and the guards' bad-smell logic, but he says his responsibility ends at the gate and confirms that the bargainer was a mortal human wizard.

After Lord Endless Ending steps aside with the badger butler, the party privately debates whether retrieving the glass key aligns with Holda's original mission, whether helping Holda is acceptable, whether the neighbor's house could be an exit, and whether any promise to a fey lord would be dangerously binding. They inspect the grandfather clock closely, notice Dwarven Reckoning numbers and a resemblance to the clock in Dandelion House, and decide to ask one last question about Holda. Lord Endless Ending says Holda came as a fool, that he sent him away rather than onward, and that once a mortal leaves the grounds they are no longer his guest. Kaito then carefully secures permission to leave toward the neighbor's house without promising to recover the key, and the Lord clarifies that if the doorway to the 27th room is truly their destination, his gates are open and he does not wish to compel them.

At the gate, the party clarifies that the exit will exist only if they have already gone through the neighbor's door in their future, making their choice feel story-bound rather than merely intentional. Txarro asks the road guards where bad-smelling travelers come from, and they say some have been seen running from the direction of the other house. Edric's concern about the clock sends the party back to Lord Endless Ending, who says the wizard and her entourage brought the clock, that one member of the entourage remains in the library, and that the clock is immovable and indestructible. Kaito reads the sleeping scholar Monetheus's thoughts, finding dreams of numbers, deadlines, unfamiliar language, and anger at his boss, while Tarek reveals books and papers taken from his satchel. With Ayveen's Comprehend Languages, the party reads The Shape of Possibility and Brilliant Numbers, learning that the clock's changing numbers appear in the book and may encode possibility, time, or coordinates in a way none of them can fully parse.

Still unsettled by the clock and the need to go home, Txarro dives into the river beside the manor and swims downriver, only to round the bend and see the same ending party again before reappearing near where he started. After the clock strikes zero, the party waits near the gate and decides to follow whoever is sent next; Tarek keeps the scholar's books, and the group debates waking him before he appears on his own, carrying a disappointingly light satchel. The scholar hails them in a language none of them can speak, but Ayveen's comprehension and his own translation ring bridge the gap. He explains that he is from Drankor, came from the 27th house with a large group, drank at the party, fell asleep, and woke to find his companions gone.

When the party asks when Monetheus is from, he realizes Drankor has been gone for centuries and that Telestria may have been right about the empire's end. He explains that his society works to maintain the porous walls between the world, the Feywild, dream, and other spaces, and that Telestria redirected their order toward closing known holes as the emperor's project consumed more and more resources. He names his master as the wizard Telestria and his order as Erythromos Limperum, then launches into brilliant numbers: encoded coordinate systems that can hide maps of weak points between spaces in plain sight. Tarek slips Monetheus's book back into his satchel, Monetheus admits he is a guide rather than a containment worker, and he says he came with a large group in the year that should have been late in the empire. After learning the current date and the empire's fall, he presses onward toward the 27th house; the party follows him down the north road to a westward footpath and a single door on a promontory, while Lord Endless Ending speaks a distant poem urging them to bring back the key and set him free.

itemDetails.name: grandfather clock

itemDetails.name: Monetheus's satchel

itemDetails.notes: The book describes mathematical and metaphysical ways to represent possible outcomes and time.

itemDetails.name: Brilliant Numbers
itemDetails.notes: The book indexes six-digit numbers that include the numbers appearing on the clock.

sourceEntries.shortSummary: The session recaps the party's arrival at Lord Endless Ending's manor and reestablishes everyone inside the ending party after a human guest is sent away.
sourceEntries.longSummary: The session opens by recapping the root tunnel from Dandelion House, the road bodies, the road guards, the ending party, the old halflings, the Rakshasa's story, and the clock striking zero as Lord Endless Ending sends a human guest toward the neighbor's house. The scene then pauses to place each character inside the manor: Edric is near the ballroom and protected against fey, Tarek has rejoined after a sideways architectural mishap and some theft, Txarro is poised between porch and ballroom with his owl following the departing woman, Ayveen stays close to the group, and Kaito returns from trying to find a better party vibe. Lord Endless Ending's beckoning of Edric is softened into an invitation to come over shortly, giving the party time to regroup.

locationHints.context: ballroom, porch, bar room, library, and adjacent party rooms during the ending party
locationHints.notes: The beat is mostly recap and character positioning inside the manor after the clock chimes zero.

npcDetails.name: Lord Endless Ending
npcDetails.notes: He has just sent a human guest away and catches Edric's eye from the far end of the ballroom.

npcDetails.name: Lord Holda
npcDetails.notes: The recap repeats that he closed the door behind the party and likely sent them to break the bargain.

npcDetails.name: Rakshasa
npcDetails.notes: His prior story about Lord Revel, the wizard, and the naming bargain is recapped.

npcDetails.name: unnamed human woman
npcDetails.context: mortal guest sent away when the clock strikes zero
npcDetails.notes: Lord Endless Ending whispers to her and she hurries out while Txarro's owl follows.

npcDetails.name: road guards
npcDetails.notes: The recap repeats their concern with travelers who carry the bad smell.

npcDetails.name: Pandel and companion
npcDetails.notes: The recap notes their apparent displacement from Chardon near the old imperial frontier.

npcDetails.name: Monetheus
npcDetails.notes: He is only known here as a sleeping human; his name is established later in the session.

itemDetails.name: grandfather clock
itemDetails.notes: The recap and opening scene center on the clock striking zero and triggering the sending of a mortal guest.

itemDetails.name: eastern screech owl familiar
itemDetails.notes: Txarro keeps the owl on the departing human woman.

itemDetails.name: Tarek's museum keys
itemDetails.notes: The recap notes that the Rakshasa accepted some of Tarek's keys as payment for information.

itemDetails.name: Dandelion House deed
itemDetails.notes: The old deed and Holda's claim are part of the recap of why the party is in the Feywild.

recapBlocks.title: Planning The Approach
sourceShortSummaries: The party compares what they know about the clock, the bargain, the road bodies, and the neighbor's house before deciding to approach Lord Endless Ending.
sourceLongSummaries: Kaito asks what everyone learned while he was away from the Rakshasa conversation, and the party pieces together the working theory: when the clock chimes zero, Lord Endless Ending must send a mortal guest toward his neighbor's house, which may explain the headless bodies and the bad smell on the road. Txarro watches through his owl as the newly sent human woman heads north toward a western promontory rather than back toward the root house. The group reviews the naming contest that turned Lord Revel into Lord Endless Ending, the wizard neighbor's role, Holda's suspicious deed and elf contact, and the danger of the road guards, then decides to speak with Lord Endless Ending and improvise suitable gifts.

sourceEntries.title: Planning The Approach

recapBlocks.title: Gifts For Endless Ending
sourceShortSummaries: The party is formally presented to Lord Endless Ending and wins his welcome with carefully improvised mortal gifts.
sourceLongSummaries: The party crosses the ballroom to Lord Endless Ending, an elegant, masked-seeming lord attended by an anxious badger butler. Kaito reads the butler's surface thoughts and finds a household servant desperate to clean, manage the house, and be released from waiting at his lord's side. The badger presents the group and asks what gifts they have brought; Txarro offers an eerie loon call from the mortal wilds, Edric awkwardly but sincerely gives holy water blessed by the Night Queen, Kaito turns a musical flourish into a showy song, Tarek performs a sleight-of-hand trick with a mouse skull, and Ayveen offers decorated sheet music for a moonrise composition. Lord Endless Ending answers each gift with poetry or judgment and finally welcomes them into his home, noting that he has rarely had the chance to greet mortals.

sourceEntries.title: Gifts For Endless Ending

recapBlocks.title: The Twenty-Seventh Room
sourceShortSummaries: Lord Endless Ending explains the neighbor's door, the zero-chiming clock, the 27th room, and the glass key that could undo his bargain.
sourceLongSummaries: Txarro admits that the party is trapped in the realm, and Lord Endless Ending says there is a way home through his nearest neighbors' house to the north, the same direction the woman was sent. When the party connects her departure to the clock, the Lord confirms that when the grandfather clock strikes zero, it is time to show a guest home through the neighbor's house. He says he was tricked by a mortal wizard into an agreement he did not intend: to send mortal guests through that house whenever the clock strikes zero. Through portents he has learned of a cold-iron 27th room locked with a glass key holding the last light of twilight; if the key is returned to him and broken, the bargain will be undone. The party raises the road bodies and the guards' bad-smell logic, but he says his responsibility ends at the gate and confirms that the bargainer was a mortal human wizard.

sourceEntries.title: The Twenty-Seventh Room

recapBlocks.title: No Fey Bargains
sourceShortSummaries: The party refuses to bind itself to a fey bargain while still choosing to leave for the neighbor's house.
sourceLongSummaries: After Lord Endless Ending steps aside with the badger butler, the party privately debates whether retrieving the glass key aligns with Holda's original mission, whether helping Holda is acceptable, whether the neighbor's house could be an exit, and whether any promise to a fey lord would be dangerously binding. They inspect the grandfather clock closely, notice Dwarven Reckoning numbers and a resemblance to the clock in Dandelion House, and decide to ask one last question about Holda. Lord Endless Ending says Holda came as a fool, that he sent him away rather than onward, and that once a mortal leaves the grounds they are no longer his guest. Kaito then carefully secures permission to leave toward the neighbor's house without promising to recover the key, and the Lord clarifies that if the doorway to the 27th room is truly their destination, his gates are open and he does not wish to compel them.

recapBlocks.title: Clockwork And Number Dreams
sourceShortSummaries: Before leaving, the party learns that the gate depends on their future choice and investigates the sleeping scholar's number-dreams and books.
sourceLongSummaries: At the gate, the party clarifies that the exit will exist only if they have already gone through the neighbor's door in their future, making their choice feel story-bound rather than merely intentional. Txarro asks the road guards where bad-smelling travelers come from, and they say some have been seen running from the direction of the other house. Edric's concern about the clock sends the party back to Lord Endless Ending, who says the wizard and her entourage brought the clock, that one member of the entourage remains in the library, and that the clock is immovable and indestructible. Kaito reads the sleeping scholar Monetheus's thoughts, finding dreams of numbers, deadlines, unfamiliar language, and anger at his boss, while Tarek reveals books and papers taken from his satchel. With Ayveen's Comprehend Languages, the party reads The Shape of Possibility and Brilliant Numbers, learning that the clock's changing numbers appear in the book and may encode possibility, time, or coordinates in a way none of them can fully parse.

recapBlocks.title: The River And Scholar
sourceShortSummaries: Txarro tests the manor's river loop, and the party meets the awakened scholar Monetheus as he is sent toward the 27th house.
sourceLongSummaries: Still unsettled by the clock and the need to go home, Txarro dives into the river beside the manor and swims downriver, only to round the bend and see the same ending party again before reappearing near where he started. After the clock strikes zero, the party waits near the gate and decides to follow whoever is sent next; Tarek keeps the scholar's books, and the group debates waking him before he appears on his own, carrying a disappointingly light satchel. The scholar hails them in a language none of them can speak, but Ayveen's comprehension and his own translation ring bridge the gap. He explains that he is from Drankor, came from the 27th house with a large group, drank at the party, fell asleep, and woke to find his companions gone.

sourceEntries.title: The River And Scholar

recapBlocks.title: Monetheus And The Door
locationRefs: single door on the western promontory
sourceShortSummaries: Monetheus realizes he is centuries out of time, explains Telestria's boundary work and brilliant-number maps, and leads the party toward the lone door.
sourceLongSummaries: When the party asks when Monetheus is from, he realizes Drankor has been gone for centuries and that Telestria may have been right about the empire's end. He explains that his society works to maintain the porous walls between the world, the Feywild, dream, and other spaces, and that Telestria redirected their order toward closing known holes as the emperor's project consumed more and more resources. He names his master as the wizard Telestria and his order as Erythromos Limperum, then launches into brilliant numbers: encoded coordinate systems that can hide maps of weak points between spaces in plain sight. Tarek slips Monetheus's book back into his satchel, Monetheus admits he is a guide rather than a containment worker, and he says he came with a large group in the year that should have been late in the empire. After learning the current date and the empire's fall, he presses onward toward the 27th house; the party follows him down the north road to a westward footpath and a single door on a promontory, while Lord Endless Ending speaks a distant poem urging them to bring back the key and set him free.
